overview: I want to serve in the Navy with a path toward special operations. My primary goal is to become a Navy SEAL, and potentially reach DEVGRU (SEAL Team 6). If I do not make SEAL, I have backup paths through SWCC, Master-at-Arms, or Navy EOD.

entry_requirements.contract: SEAL Challenge Contract — guarantees a BUD/S slot

path_nodes.label: Navy Boot Camp — Recruit Training Command
path_nodes.description: I enter the Navy as an E-1. Eight weeks of physical conditioning, military customs, seamanship fundamentals. I am flagged for NSW pipeline from day one if I have a SEAL Challenge Contract.

life_layer.financial: Save every dollar; no car payment until after BUD/S — attrition is 75%+
life_layer.marriage: Strong recommendation: do NOT marry before BUD/S completes

path_nodes.label: Naval Special Warfare Preparatory School
path_nodes.description: 8-week course bridging boot camp to BUD/S standards. Swimming, running, calisthenics, and NSW mentorship from active SEALs. No BUD/S-style harassment — pure preparation. If I fail PST standards here I am reclassed.

path_nodes.label: BUD/S Orientation
path_nodes.description: Three-week introduction to Coronado, O-Course, BUD/S PT standards, and NSW culture. Last graceful exit point before First Phase.

path_nodes.label: BUD/S First Phase — Basic Conditioning (Hell Week)
path_nodes.description: Seven weeks of progressively demanding PT: timed 4-mile runs, 2-mile swims, O-Course, log PT. Week 4 = Hell Week: 5.5 days, ~4 hours total sleep, 200+ miles of movement, 20+ hours PT/day.
path_nodes.attrition: ~67% of those reaching Phase 1 do not finish; most quit before or during Hell Week

path_nodes.label: BUD/S Second Phase — Combat Diving
path_nodes.description: Seven weeks of open-circuit SCUBA, closed-circuit LAR V Draeger rebreather, underwater search/recovery, hydrographic recon, and combat swimming. Attrition drops sharply here.
path_nodes.attrition: ~13% who enter Phase 2 do not complete it

path_nodes.label: BUD/S Third Phase — Land Warfare Training
path_nodes.location: NAB Coronado + San Clemente Island, CA
path_nodes.description: Weapons, demolitions, land nav, patrolling, rappelling, marksmanship, small-unit tactics. Second half on San Clemente Island (60 miles from Coronado) for live-fire exercises. I graduate BUD/S as a Special Warfare Operator — NOT yet a SEAL.
path_nodes.attrition: ~4% who reach Phase 3 do not complete it

path_nodes.label: SEAL Qualification Training (SQT)
path_nodes.description: 26 weeks of advanced tactical training: mission planning, intel gathering, land warfare (3 weeks Niland CA or Camp A.P. Hill), maritime ops, cold weather, CQC, advanced shooting. I earn my Trident at SQT graduation. Total pipeline from contract: ~30 months.
special_tracks: Selected graduates: 9-month SOF Medic course (18D equivalent) at Stennis, MS
SDV School for Seal Delivery Vehicle operations

path_nodes.label: First SEAL Team Tour
path_nodes.location: East Coast: Virginia Beach, VA / West Coast: Coronado, CA
path_nodes.description: I report to a SEAL Team (Teams 1-7 or SDV Teams). Platoon cycle = 18-24 months: workup, deployment, stand-down. Typically 2 deployments minimum at first team. This is where I become a true operator.
qualifications_available: NSW Sniper Course (~80 days, Dam Neck VA) — one of the most demanding sniper courses in DoD

special_pays.combat_zone_tax_exclusion: Full base pay tax-free in combat zone

life_layer.housing: BAH if married ($1,800-2,800/month Virginia Beach or Coronado); barracks if single
life_layer.college: TA ($4,500/yr) — 1-2 online classes/semester during garrison only; impossible during deployment or workup
life_layer.financial: Max TSP. Build 6-month emergency fund using combat zone tax-free pay. Avoid truck/motorcycle debt trap.
life_layer.marriage: Most SEALs marry during first tour. Divorce rate is high in NSW due to op tempo and secrecy. Be honest with partner about lifestyle before committing.
life_layer.kids: Possible but challenging during 6-9 month deployments

decision_gate.question: After 5 years total service and 2 deployments, what is my next move?

options.requires: 5 yrs SEAL Team service, 2 deployments, clean record

options.choice: Stay in SEAL Teams as senior NCO/leader
Take a B-billet (instructor, recruiter, joint assignment)

path_nodes.label: Naval Special Warfare Sniper Course
path_nodes.description: ~80-day course covering long-range marksmanship, field craft, stalking, ballistics, wind calls, surveillance, and target detection. One of the most demanding sniper courses in the U.S. military. Open to SEALs after first deployment. High attrition. Qualification adds to my team value and operator profile.
path_nodes.note: Qualification course — I remain on my SEAL Team and add sniper to my skill set. Tracked alongside MARSOC/Army sniper school as comparable credential.

path_nodes.label: DEVGRU Green Team — SEAL Team 6 Selection
path_nodes.location: Dam Neck Annex, Virginia Beach, VA

eligibility.rank_range: E-4 to E-8 enlisted / O-3 to O-4 officer
eligibility.record: No NJP, civil conviction, or alcohol incidents in past 3 years
eligibility.swim: Must complete modified 2nd Class Swim Qual after 35-hour NSW Swim COI

path_nodes.how_to_apply: Email NSWDG-Recruiting@socom.mil — include name, rate, PRD, current command
path_nodes.description: Green Team is the 6-month assessment and selection for DEVGRU. I am under constant instructor observation. I am NOT yet a DEVGRU member during Green Team — I am auditioning. Roughly 50% of candidates are cut.

path_nodes.label: DEVGRU Operator — Tier 1 Special Mission Unit
path_nodes.location: Dam Neck Annex, Virginia Beach, VA
path_nodes.description: I am now in one of the most elite units in the world. DEVGRU has assault squadrons (Gold, Red, Blue, Silver, Gray), support elements, and development/testing components. Cycle: 4 months training, 4 months global standby (immediate response anywhere), 4 months second-phase training.
path_nodes.retirement_note: At 20 years I can retire with pension, TRICARE for life, VA benefits. At E-8 with 20 years: ~$2,400+/month pension for life.

options.choice: Continue to 25-30 years in senior leadership or Warrant Officer path

path_nodes.label: Senior SEAL NCO / Team Leader / Chief
path_nodes.description: I become a platoon chief, team LCPO, lead instructor at BUD/S, or joint SOF assignment leader. I mentor junior SEALs, shape training programs, and cycle through multiple teams and B-billets toward 20 years.

options.note: Builds communication skills; bridge to civilian career

options.location: Pearl Harbor HI or Virginia Beach VA

path_nodes.label: Navy Reclass — Did Not Complete SEAL Pipeline
path_nodes.description: ~75-80% of all SEAL candidates do not complete the pipeline. This is not a failure of character. I still have a full Navy career with enormous opportunity.

reclass_options.description: Elite NSW boat operators working alongside SEALs. Direct action, riverine, coastal ops.
Navy military police/force protection. Path to MSRON, MSRT, or NCIS.
Explosive Ordnance Disposal — highly technical, deploys with SOF, second-longest training pipeline in DoD.
Navy medic embedded with Marines. Fleet Marine Force Corpsman is one of the most respected Navy billets.
Imagery and signals intelligence working closely with SOF units.
Electronics, aviation, nuclear, IT, logistics, culinary — enormous breadth of options.

path_nodes.label: 20-Year Retirement
path_nodes.description: I retire with a monthly pension for life, TRICARE for life, VA benefits, and 20+ years of skills the civilian sector values highly.
civilian_paths: Federal law enforcement (FBI, DEA, USMS, DHS) — NSW background extremely competitive
Defense contractor (Booz Allen, SAIC, L3Harris, Leidos)
Finance/private equity (MBA path via Honor Foundation)

swcc_standalone_path.label: SWCC Path — Special Warfare Combatant-craft Crewman
swcc_standalone_path.overview: If I want NSW community without the SEAL path, SWCC is a direct-contract option. Elite boat operators executing direct action, SEAL insertion/extraction, and maritime intelligence.
swcc_standalone_path.advancement: Senior SWCCs become Warrant Officers commanding Special Boat Teams

college.TA: $4,500/yr, max 18 credit hours/yr — use during shore duty and garrison, not deployments
college.CLEP_DANTES: Free credit-by-exam for active duty — knock out 30 gen-ed credits at zero cost
college.GI_Bill_post_service: 36 months full tuition + BAH stipend at public university

financial_rules: No car payment above 10% of take-home pay
TSP from Day 1 — minimum 5% to capture BRS match
6-month emergency fund before any major purchase
VA Loan: zero down, no PMI — use it
Bank combat zone tax-free pay aggressively
life_layer.injury_floor: By 24: 6+ months savings, 30+ college credits, zero high-interest debt — regardless of what happens to my military path
